history.content:
<thought>
The goal G is visible in the upper right direction on the map. Directly in front of me is a floor cell '.', which is safe. I should move forward toward the goal while avoiding the lava cells to my right.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
Now I am at the bottom-center again, facing forward. In front of me is a floor cell '.', but to my right there is lava. To avoid stepping into lava, I should advance forward first as it is safe before trying to navigate towards the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am now at the bottom-center again, facing forward. In front of me is a free floor cell '.', and the goal G is two cells to the right. To get closer to the goal, I should move forward first and then plan to turn right towards the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I just moved forward and am at the bottom-center facing forward. The goal G is visible two cells to the right on the same row. I need to turn right to face the goal before moving towards it.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am currently at the bottom-center facing right. The goal G is now immediately to my left one cell away. To reach the goal, I need to turn left to face it and then move forward.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal G is two cells ahead and one cell to the right. I am currently facing forward, but the path directly forward has a wall (#) in front of me. I need to turn right to navigate towards the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing forward at the bottom-center. The goal G is to my left, and the path to the left seems clear. I should turn left to face the goal directly and proceed.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I see that the goal G is two cells ahead and one cell to the right, but there is a wall # directly in front of me. I must turn right to move around the wall towards the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing forward and the goal G is visible to my left. The cell directly in front is clear floor. Since the goal is towards the left, I will turn left to face that direction and head towards the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am currently facing forward with a wall directly in front of me, so moving forward is not possible. The goal G is two cells ahead and one cell to the right, so I will turn right to maneuver towards the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal G is to my left. I am facing forward and the space ahead is clear. To move towards the goal safely, I should turn left first to face the goal direction.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing forward, and the position directly ahead is a wall, so I cannot move forward. The goal G is visible to my right. To approach the goal, I should turn right first.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal G is to my left, but there is lava (L) directly to the left, which is dangerous. The path ahead is clear, so I will move forward safely to avoid the lava while trying to get closer to the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
There is lava directly in front of me, so moving forward is not safe. The goal G is to my left, so I will turn left to face the goal and avoid the lava ahead.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal G is two cells directly to my right. The cell in front is a wall (#), so I cannot move forward. I will turn right to face the goal and then move forward in the next action.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>
